diagrams.title: Basic Python Class Structure for IoT Device
diagrams.description: A structural diagram showing the anatomy of a Python class designed to represent an IoT device
elements: Class definition with 'class' keyword and class name
__init__ constructor method with 'self' parameter
Instance attributes (device_id, name, status, location)
Methods (connect, read_data, disconnect)
Self keyword connecting to instance
diagrams.purpose: Students learn the fundamental structure of Python classes including constructor, attributes, and methods - essential for modeling IoT devices as software objects

diagrams.title: IoT Device Class Inheritance Hierarchy
diagrams.description: A hierarchical diagram showing how specialized IoT device classes inherit from a base Device class
elements: Base class: IoTDevice with common attributes (device_id, status, location) and methods (connect, disconnect)
TemperatureSensor subclass inheriting from IoTDevice, adding sensor_type and temperature attributes
HumiditySensor subclass with humidity attribute
Actuator base class with action method
LED Actuator and Motor Actuator subclasses
Arrows showing inheritance relationships with 'inherits from' label
diagrams.purpose: Students understand inheritance in Python - how specialized device types inherit properties from base classes and can override or extend functionality for IoT applications

diagrams.title: Class Instance Creation Flow
diagrams.description: A flowchart showing how a class blueprint creates multiple device instances in IoT
elements: Class Definition (Blueprint) box with IoT device template
Arrow pointing to 'Instantiation' process
Device Instance 1 (Sensor Kitchen), Device Instance 2 (Sensor Bedroom), Device Instance 3 (Sensor Garage) as separate objects
Each instance showing 'self' reference and unique attribute values
Memory representation showing instances stored in variables
diagrams.purpose: Students visualize the relationship between class definitions and their instances, understanding that one class can create multiple independent device objects with different states

diagrams.title: Encapsulation in IoT Device Classes
diagrams.description: A diagram demonstrating encapsulation concepts with public and private attributes in an IoT context
elements: IoTDevice class as a container
Public attributes: device_name, location (accessible anywhere)
Protected attribute: _status (convention - accessible within class and subclasses)
Private attribute: __connection_string (name mangling - only accessible within class)
Getter and Setter methods for controlled access
Arrow showing external code accessing only through public methods
diagrams.purpose: Students learn encapsulation principles - protecting internal device state while providing controlled interfaces, essential for maintaining IoT device integrity and security

charts.title: Functions vs Classes in IoT Context
charts.content: Comparison table showing when to use functions versus classes: Functions for simple, one-time operations (convert_temperature); Classes for IoT devices with persistent state (sensor objects that maintain readings over time), multiple related data attributes, and behaviors that operate on that data

charts.title: Python Class Key Terminology
charts.content: Table defining: Class (blueprint), Object/Instance (actual device), Attribute (characteristic like temperature), Method (behavior like read()), Constructor/__init__ (initialization), Self (reference to current instance), Inheritance (child class), Encapsulation (data hiding)

charts.title: Creating an IoT Device Class Step-by-Step
charts.content: Flowchart: Define class name → Write __init__ constructor → Add instance attributes for device properties → Define methods for device behaviors → Create instances with unique values → Call methods on instances to interact with IoT devices

charts.title: Access Modifiers in Python IoT Classes
charts.content: Comparison of attribute/method visibility: Public (no prefix - accessible everywhere), Protected (_ prefix - accessible in class and subclasses), Private (__ prefix - name mangling, class-only access) with IoT examples like public device_name, protected _battery_level, private __api_key

mindMaps.purpose: Comprehensive mind map showing all aspects of Python classes relevant to IoT development - from basic syntax to practical applications with popular IoT frameworks
